questions.question: Define Chomsky Normal Form and state its importance in the theory of computation.
keyPoints: CNF is a form of context-free grammar where productions are either A → BC or A → a
Start symbol cannot appear on RHS
No unit productions (A → B) allowed
No ε-productions except possibly S → ε
Important for CYK algorithm, decidability proofs, and parsing

questions.question: What is a unit production? How is it eliminated while converting a CFG to CNF?
keyPoints: Unit production is A → B where both A and B are variables
To eliminate: for each unit production A → B, add all non-unit productions of B to A
Remove all unit productions after this process
This ensures all productions lead directly to terminals

questions.question: Explain the term 'nullable variable' in context-free grammars. How are ε-productions handled during CNF conversion?
keyPoints: Nullable variable derives ε (empty string)
Identify all nullable variables first
For each production containing nullable variables, create new productions by removing them in all combinations
Finally add S → ε if start symbol is nullable

questions.question: What is a useless symbol? How do you remove useless symbols from a grammar?
keyPoints: Useless symbol: not generating or not reachable from start symbol
Generating: can derive some terminal string
Reachable: can appear in some derivation from start symbol
Remove non-generating symbols first, then unreachable ones

questions.question: State and explain the four conditions that a grammar must satisfy to be in Chomsky Normal Form.
keyPoints: Every production is either A → BC or A → a
Start symbol S does not appear on RHS of any production
No unit productions (A → B) exist
No ε-productions (except possibly S → ε)

questions.question: Convert the grammar G = {S → aSb | ε} to Chomsky Normal Form.
keyPoints: First eliminate ε-production S → ε
This makes S nullable
Add new productions to replace combinations with S
Convert terminals in multi-symbol productions to variables
Final CNF form will have new variables for terminals

questions.question: Explain the steps involved in converting a Context-Free Grammar to Chomsky Normal Form.
keyPoints: Step 1: Remove ε-productions by identifying nullable variables
Step 2: Remove unit productions using closure method
Step 3: Remove useless symbols (non-generating and unreachable)
Step 4: Convert terminals in RHS with multiple symbols to variables
Step 5: Ensure RHS has at most 2 variables

questions.question: Convert the following CFG to Chomsky Normal Form: S → AB, A → aA | a, B → bB | b. Show all intermediate steps.
keyPoints: Step 1: Grammar already has no ε-productions or unit productions
Step 2: Check for useless symbols - all symbols are generating and reachable
Step 3: For A → aA and B → bB, introduce new variables: X → a, Y → b
Step 4: Convert to CNF: S → AB, A → XA | a, B → YB | b, X → a, Y → b
Final CNF: S → AB, A → XA | X, B → YB | Y, X → a, Y → b

questions.question: What is the CYK algorithm? Explain why it requires the grammar to be in CNF. Also, state the time complexity of CYK algorithm.
keyPoints: CYK (Cocke-Younger-Kasami) is a parsing algorithm for context-free languages
Uses dynamic programming to check membership of a string
Requires CNF because it works with productions of form A → BC
This allows binary splitting of substrings into two parts
Time complexity is O(n³ × |P|) where n is string length and |P| is number of productions
With proper CNF, it efficiently determines if string can be derived

questions.question: Explain the concept of useless symbols in grammars. Given a grammar G with productions: S → AB, A → a, B → bC, C → c, identify and remove all useless symbols.
keyPoints: Useless symbols: non-generating (cannot derive terminal string) or unreachable (cannot be derived from S)
Finding generating symbols: start with terminals, work backwards through productions
Finding reachable symbols: start from S, mark all symbols reachable
In given grammar: S → AB, A → a (generating), B → bC (needs C), C → c (generating, so B is generating)
All symbols are generating and reachable, so none are useless
However, if grammar had S → AB and A → a only, B would be non-generating

questions.question: Differentiate between Chomsky Normal Form and Greibach Normal Form. Also explain why both forms are useful in parsing context-free languages.
keyPoints: CNF: Productions are A → BC (two variables) or A → a (single terminal)
GNF: Productions are A → aα where a is terminal and α is variables (possibly empty)
CNF useful for CYK algorithm and proving decidability
GNF useful for top-down parsing as it gives clear choice of production
GNF eliminates left recursion in a specific way
Both simplify grammar analysis and parsing algorithms
